items.text:
Each of discovery.md's six forks (a-f) is resolved in decisions.md with a
one-line rationale tied to the constitution (does it buy back time? stays
diffable? minimal deps?).

The go/no-go is made explicit: a stated judgement that JSON-backed state
buys back more time than its ceremony costs, or the workstream is cut here.
(decisions.md: GO)

plan.md created in the living-plan schema: ordered P-items (each a binary
DoD sketch + committed|optional tag) covering schema+validation, the
discovery verbs, rewiring mutating verbs to JSON+view-regen, and migrating
this workstream (dogfood), with sequencing rationale. (P1-P6)

The first execution iteration is concrete enough to open next (its slice
named and DoD sketched). (P1: schemas + validator)

handback:
Resolved all six discovery forks in decisions.md (GO; one state.json per
workstream; vendored stdlib validator; converter over all workstreams; DoD stays
artifact; auto view-regen; state behind the Repository seam). Built plan.md as a
living plan: P1 schemas+validator, P2 state.json I/O behind Repository, P3
migration converter (dogfoods this workstream), P4 rewire mutating verbs to
JSON+view-regen, P5 discovery verbs (entities/schema/get), P6 retro+close - with
dependency-driven sequencing and three deferred items. Snapshot's Next now
derives from the plan. First execution slice (P1) is concrete enough to open
next. No engine code touched.
self_review:
Adversarial pass against the DoD:
- All six forks resolved, each rationale tied to a named constraint (buy-back,
  diffability, minimal deps, replaceability) rather than preference. Two are
  deliberate *cuts* (DoD-as-data, standalone render verb) - planning that only
  adds is a smell; this one trims.
- The go/no-go is a real judgement, not a rubber stamp: it rests on discovery's
  finding that state is already isolated + snapshot already derives, so the cost
  is bounded. If discovery had found state tangled through prose, the honest call
  would have been no-go.
- Plan sequencing is dependency-driven (schema -> I/O -> migrate -> rewire ->
  read verbs -> close), and migration is placed *before* the verb rewire on
  purpose so P4 runs against real JSON and the dogfood workstream is already
  converted. P1 is concrete enough to open immediately.
- Risk: P4 (rewire) is the largest item and where existing tests will churn;
  flagged so it can be split if it overflows one iteration. P3's converter must
  be idempotent or re-runs corrupt state - called out in its sketch.
- Scope held: no engine code; every item is deferred to its execution iteration.
